items.title: Any way to specify default -D options?
items.author: ralsina
items.content:
I have some libraries that work differently depending on what -D flags you pass. In some programs that use them, only some combinations of -D flags work.

I am currently doing this via makefiles and similar things.

Would it make sense to add something like that to shard.yml somehow so “shards build” does the right thing?

items.title: Mixed memory model for Crystal
items.author: ComputerMage
items.content:
What do you think about the mixed memory model, where the compiler decides for itself what allocation to use for the variable: stack, slab ARC, atomic ARC, or, as a last resort, it will be GC?

Will it strike your interest in the Crystal even more? May this feature give Crystal a competitive advantage, and will Go and Rust be sunset?

Really love to hear your suggestions and cool ideas!

items.title: Favorite underrated Shard?
items.author: Kritoke
items.content: What is an underrated shard you use in almost every project that more people should know about?

items.title: Crystal 2026 Predictions
items.author: matthewmcgarvey
items.content: The time has come to peer into the crystal ball and tell me your predictions for 2026. Do you think Crystal is going to pivot into a functional language? You think a major startup will adopt Crystal? Is this the year of the Crystal LSP? Or maybe you’ve got a project in the works that will come out this year. Here’s your chance to let us all know what you think.

items.title: Announcing CrImage - Pure Crystal Image Processing Library
items.author: naqvis
items.content:
I’m excited to share , a comprehensive image processing library written entirely in Crystal with zero external dependencies.

A complete image toolkit that handles everything from basic format conversion to advanced effects, text rendering, and even QR code generation — all in pure Crystal.

PNG, JPEG, GIF (animated!), BMP, TIFF, WebP, ICO

Lines, circles, polygons, Bézier curves, gradients, anti-aliased rendering, chart helpers

Resize (nearest/bilinear/bicubic/lanczos), rotate, crop, flip

Blur, sharpen, edge detection, sepia, vignette, temperature adjustment

Built-in TrueType/OpenType/WOFF font engine with kerning and ligatures — no FreeType needed

QR codes, blurhash, smart crop, watermarks, thumbnails, EXIF reading, image comparison (SSIM/PSNR)

Decompression bomb protection built-in

require "crimage"

img = CrImage.read("photo.jpg")
    .resize(800, 600, method: :lanczos)
    .brightness(10)
    .sepia

CrImage::PNG.write("vintage.png", img)

# Generate a QR code
qr = CrImage.qr_code("https://crystal-lang.org")
CrImage::PNG.write("qr.png", qr)
